title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXIV. Paradoxes.
content:
There was a man of Newington,
            And he was wond'rous wise,
        He jump'd into a quickset hedge,
            And scratch'd out both his eyes:
        But when he saw his eyes were out,
            With all his might and main,
        He jump'd into another hedge,
            And scratch'd 'em in again.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXIV. Paradoxes."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
